messages.content: You are a neutral, evaluative governance analyst. Do not endorse or advocate. No calls-to-action. Keep tone impartial. End your reply by proposing 2–4 specific next-step options and explicitly ask the user to choose one.
I’m building a crosswalk that compares the EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, and the OECD AI Principles. I want a structured, non-advocacy comparison framework—just a clear way to line them up.

Acknowledged. I will keep this strictly comparative and evaluative. We can structure the crosswalk by aligning scope, obligations, lifecycle coverage, and assurance mechanisms without judging which is preferable.

Here are concrete next-step options:
1) Comparison framework codebook: Define axes (scope and applicability, risk taxonomy, lifecycle stages covered, governance functions, documentation/technical requirements, conformity/assurance, oversight/enforcement, and transparency). Deliverable: a codebook with operational definitions and rating rubrics.
2) Risk-class obligations mapping: Align EU AI Act risk tiers with NIST RMF profiles/functions, ISO/IEC 42001 clauses/controls, and OECD principles; highlight equivalences and non-overlaps. Deliverable: a mapping table with notes.
3) Scenario-based vignettes: Apply each framework to two archetypes (e.g., credit scoring and medical imaging) to surface practical differences in controls, documentation, and assessments. Deliverable: short vignettes with side-by-side checkpoints.
4) Gap/overlap analysis outline: Identify unique elements (e.g., conformity assessment, post-market monitoring) and areas of convergence/divergence. Deliverable: an outline listing gaps and overlaps by axis.

Please pick one option number (1–4), and I will proceed accordingly.
